description: Renovate configuration for Proxmox infrastructure automation (Terraform, Docker Compose, Ansible)
dependencyDashboardHeader:
Tracks dependency updates for Proxmox infrastructure automation.

**After merging**: Test changes in staging before applying to production. See repo README for deployment procedures.

---

customManagers.description: Extract tool versions from Dockerfile ARG variables with Renovate annotations
Extract Unbound version from the unbound + unbound-recursive Dockerfiles (kept in lockstep)
Extract OpenSSL version from the unbound + unbound-recursive Dockerfiles (kept in lockstep)
Extract Debian base image version from the unbound + unbound-recursive Dockerfile ARGs (kept in lockstep)
Extract Debian base image version+digest from docker/dnscrypt-proxy/Dockerfile ARG
Extract Terraform provider versions from Terragrunt root.hcl heredoc
Extract Talos version from Ansible group vars (Talos cluster only; the ansible/lxc inventory carries no version keys).
Extract Kubernetes version from Ansible group vars (Talos cluster only; the ansible/lxc inventory carries no version keys).

packageRules.description: Infrastructure updates - graduated stabilization soak by update type (patch 1d). Trusted vendor releases (Talos, Kubernetes, Terraform providers, Docker images, Ansible collections) still land quickly, but a short soak guards against same-day broken releases; CI catches breakage before merge. The .ci/docker/Dockerfile build base keeps its own 3/7/14-day delays from the shared preset. Critical OS/cluster deps (Talos, Kubernetes) keep their own rules below.
Infrastructure updates - minor soak.
Infrastructure updates - major soak.
Docker digest pinning - Pin floating tags to reproducible digests for infrastructure reliability
Docker Compose service images - Grouped by service directory
Custom Docker base images - Build validation
Debian base image for Unbound - Disable built-in dockerfile manager (handled by custom regex)
Debian base image for Unbound - Base OS updates
Unbound DNS resolver version - Core DNS service
OpenSSL library version - Security library used by Unbound. Pinned to <4 until NLnet Labs ships an unbound release with OpenSSL 4.x support
dnscrypt-proxy version - Encrypted DNS proxy binary

Terraform unit versions.tf - Disabled because Terragrunt regenerates these from root.hcl on every init (root.hcl is the source of truth, tracked by the customManager above). The single-level glob deliberately excludes the hand-written modules/*/versions.tf, which Renovate DOES manage.

Terraform providers - Grouped updates for IaC validation. root.hcl is the source of truth for unit-level provider constraints (the unit versions.tf are regenerated from it); the hand-written modules/*/versions.tf carry their own constraints and are bumped in the same group so they stay aligned.
Ansible Galaxy collections - Configuration management
Talos OS version - Critical cluster OS updates

Kubernetes version - Capped at v1.35.x. Active cluster is Talos 1.13.3 (k8s v1.35.5), which the cap fits; the bound also keeps headroom for the Rancher 2.14 support matrix (1.33-1.35) should the Rancher/K3s path be brought back from .archive/.

CI Docker base image - Group with CI pipeline Docker versions for atomic updates. pinDigests:false (NOT scoped to one manager): the docker base ARG carries tag@sha256 and is seen by BOTH the native dockerfile manager (FROM docker:${DOCKER_VERSION}) and the custom.regex annotation; with pinDigests:true the inherited docker:pinDigests probes a malformed `manifests/<tag>@sha256:<digest>` HEAD that 404s ('Could not determine new digest'). Applying pinDigests:false to every manager for depName docker suppresses the probe; a real tag bump still rewrites tag+digest via the autoReplaceStringTemplate, so the digest stays current.

CI/CD components - track the homelab/ci-components @version pins in the include block. The gitlabci manager collapses every `component: source.example.com/homelab/ci-components/<name>@<ver>` line to a single homelab/ci-components dep (gitlab-tags datasource), so one grouped MR bumps all of them together. A short soak lets a fresh component release settle; CI proves the pipeline still renders before merge.

PostgreSQL version pinning - bitwarden pinned to v16.x. The db (plain postgres) and backup (postgres-alpine) services share depName 'postgres'; they are kept on the SAME version (16.14) so Renovate doesn't hit a same-depName 'upgrade collision' (which neither grouping nor branch-splitting via matchCurrentValue resolved — it's a Renovate dedup limitation for two versions of one image in one file). Keeping them aligned is the reliable fix; this rule just holds the v16.x pin.
PostgreSQL version pinning - patchmon pinned to v17.x
PostgreSQL version pinning - paperless-ngx pinned to v18.x
Redis version pinning - patchmon pinned to v7.x
Redis version pinning - paperless-ngx pinned to v8.x

prFooter:
This MR was created automatically by Renovate. Review changes carefully before merging.

**After merging**:
- Terraform changes: Review plan output before apply
- Docker Compose: Verify service health after deployment
- Custom Docker images: Rebuild and test before deploying
